summary: Backend-only regression for SECURITY HARDENING + IMPERSONATE TENANT ADMIN. 27 tests written, 24 PASSED, 1 SKIPPED (no second-org user available in seed), 2 FAILED — both expose the SAME critical backend bug: POST /api/auth/forgot-password returns HTTP 500 on every success path because the slowapi `@limiter.limit` decorator tries to inject rate-limit headers but the endpoint signature is missing a `response: Response` parameter. Impersonation endpoint works flawlessly. All other security controls (rate limiting, brute-force, CORS, Razorpay HMAC, JWT rotation, assign_lead authorization, public widget rate limit) verified.

critical.issue: Endpoint returns HTTP 500 on every successful request (both valid and invalid emails). Root cause: slowapi's `_inject_headers` requires the endpoint to declare a `response: Response` parameter when `headers_enabled=True` is set on the Limiter, but `async def forgot_password(data, request)` is missing it. Stack trace from /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log: `Exception: parameter \`response\` must be an instance of starlette.responses.Response` at `slowapi/extension.py:382`. Net effect: password-reset feature is COMPLETELY BROKEN for end users — every request 500s and no reset email/log is ever written. The rate-limit branch (4th+ request in a minute) still returns 429 correctly because slowapi raises RateLimitExceeded before inject_headers runs, which is why the rate-limit test passed.
critical.fix: Change signature to `async def forgot_password(data: ForgotPasswordRequest, request: Request, response: Response):` (line ~1098 in server.py).

minor.issue: Same slowapi inject_headers bug likely applies to /api/auth/reset-password success path (signature also lacks `response: Response`). We could not directly exercise the success path because we have no valid reset token (forgot-password endpoint is broken). Should be fixed defensively at the same time.

Same slowapi inject_headers bug applies — endpoint declared at line ~806 with `@limiter.limit('5/hour')` but no `response: Response` parameter. Not exercised in this run, but registration will 500 on success. Verify signature.

action_items: FIX: Add `response: Response` parameter to forgot_password() at /app/backend/server.py line 1098 — endpoint currently returns 500 on every successful call (slowapi inject_headers crash).
FIX: Same defensive change for reset_password() (line 1123) and register() (line ~806) — both lack the `response: Response` arg required by `@limiter.limit` when headers are enabled.
OPTIONAL: After fix, re-run TestForgotPasswordNoLeak class — should turn both tests green and confirm user-enumeration protection works.
OPTIONAL: Seed a second-org admin/user via demo data so TestAssignLeadAuthorization::test_cross_tenant_assignment_blocked runs instead of being skipped.
critical_code_review_comments: Inconsistent slowapi signature usage across auth endpoints — login() correctly has `response: Response` but forgot-password/reset-password/register do not. Add a code-level lint / pre-commit check or a small wrapper that enforces `response: Response` whenever `@limiter.limit` is used.

/app/backend/server.py is 5357 lines — well above the 700-line guideline. Split into routers/{auth,leads,platform,billing,webhooks,widget,locations,integrations}.py for maintainability and to reduce regression blast radius for future security work.

Impersonation endpoint: audit log entry is created BEFORE the cookie is set — fine — but consider adding `ended_at` on subsequent /auth/logout to compute session duration; right now the audit only captures `started_at`.
Impersonation grants a full 30-min access_token but does NOT issue a refresh_token (correctly cleared), so super-admin must re-impersonate after 30 min — confirm this UX is intended.

CORS preflight test only validates the negative case (no wildcard with credentials). Edge layer (Cloudflare) may still allow `*` — the backend config is correct, but worth a one-time verification at the CDN.

success_rate.backend: 92.6% (24/26 executed; 1 skipped due to seed gap; 2 failed due to ONE backend bug)

test_credentials: From /app/memory/test_credentials.md — super: admin@educationcrm.com/Admin@123, org_admin: orgadmin@demo.com/Demo@123, manager/counselor/telecaller @demo.com/Demo@123
seed_data_creation: None. Tests reused existing demo data (Bright Future Coaching org, widget_token DLjmL9QIEgmT3CzWY8tceA). Cleared `login_attempts` collection at start to ensure brute-force test gets a clean slate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_security.py after main agent fixes the slowapi `response: Response` parameter bug. Expect 26/26 PASS + 1 skip (or 27/27 if a second-org user is seeded). The test file is idempotent and self-contained — it uses unique X-Forwarded-For per request and unique fake emails for brute-force, so it won't pollute production rate-limit state. Login attempts collection may need clearing again before re-run: `await db.login_attempts.delete_many({})`. Rate-limit windows are 1 minute, so consecutive full-suite runs should be spaced ~60s apart.
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/auth/forgot-password 500: slowapi's Limiter is initialised with `headers_enabled=True` (server.py line ~110). slowapi then expects the rate-limited endpoint to declare BOTH `request: Request` AND `response: Response` so it can inject `X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset` response headers after the endpoint returns. The forgot_password handler only declares `request: Request`, so on the success path slowapi's `_inject_headers()` raises `Exception: parameter \`response\` must be an instance of starlette.responses.Response`. FastAPI catches this and converts it to a 500. The rate-limit branch (429) is unaffected because RateLimitExceeded is raised BEFORE inject_headers runs. Reproduction: `curl -X POST $API/api/auth/forgot-password -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"anyone@example.com"}'` → 500. Mitigation: add `response: Response` parameter to forgot_password() (and reset_password, register) signature; no other code change needed.
